[602]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 檇李顧淵白恃才傲物 - Literally, 'Cui Yuanbai of Cui Li boasts of his talent and looks down on others.' This phrase describes someone who is proud of their abilities and disdainful of others.
  • 今大朝四海一統六合一家 - Literally, 'Now the great court has unified the four seas and the six directions as one family.' This phrase emphasizes the unity and harmony of the empire under a single ruler.
  • 燕蓋昔時戰國名 - Literally, 'Yan is the name of an ancient state during the Warring States period.' This phrase provides historical context for the name 'Yan.'
  • 切中其實 - Literally, 'strikes the truth directly.' This phrase means that something hits the mark or is very accurate.
  • 裱背十三科 - Literally, 'thirteen categories of mounting and backing.' This phrase refers to the different types of work involved in the art of mounting and backing paintings.
  • 織造綾錦絹帛 - Literally, 'weaving silk, brocade, and cloth.' This phrase describes the process of making various types of fabric.
  • 染練上件 - Literally, 'dyeing the above items.' This phrase refers to the dyeing process for the fabrics mentioned.
  • 抄造纸劄 - Literally, 'copying paper and making paper.' This phrase describes the process of making paper.
  • 染制上件顔色 - Literally, 'dyeing the above colors.' This phrase refers to the dyeing process for the colors used in the fabrics.
  • 糊料麥麪 - Literally, 'glue made from wheat flour.' This phrase describes the material used for gluing.
  • 糊药矾蜡 - Literally, 'glue made from alum and wax.' This phrase describes another type of glue used in the process.
  • 界尺裁版杆帖 - Literally, 'rulers for cutting and pasting.' This phrase describes the tools used for cutting and pasting.
  • 轴头【或金或玉或石或玛瑙水晶珊瑚沉檀花梨乌木每轴上用一色所以只归一科】 - Literally, 'axis heads (made of gold, jade, stone, agate, crystal, coral, sandalwood, rosewood, ebony, each axis head using one color, hence it is counted as one category).' This phrase describes the different materials used for the axis heads and why they are counted as one category.
  • 糊刷 - Literally, 'glue brush.' This phrase describes the tool used for applying glue.
  • 铰链 - Literally, 'hinges.' This phrase describes the hinges used in mounting.
  • 绦 - Literally, 'silk cord.' This phrase describes the cords used in mounting.
  • 经带 - Literally, 'silk tape.' This phrase describes the tapes used in mounting.
  • 裁刀 - Literally, 'cutting knife.' This phrase describes the knife used for cutting.
  • 阙其一则不能成全画矣 - Literally, 'if any one of these is missing, the painting cannot be completed.' This phrase emphasizes the importance of all the steps in the mounting process.
  • 糊刷裁尺亦皆有名 - Literally, 'the glue brushes and cutting rulers also have names.' This phrase indicates that each tool has a specific name.
  • 防软者谓之平分 - Literally, 'the soft ones are called even division.' This phrase describes a type of glue brush.
  • 防硬者谓之糊防 - Literally, 'the hard ones are called glue protection.' This phrase describes another type of glue brush.
  • 大小得中者谓之黏合 - Literally, 'the ones of moderate size are called adhesion.' This phrase describes a third type of glue brush.
  • 狭小者谓之寸金 - Literally, 'the narrow ones are called inch gold.' This phrase describes a fourth type of glue brush.
  • 裁尺极等濶者曰满手 - Literally, 'the widest ones are called full hand.' This phrase describes the widest cutting rulers.
  • 次等曰三指 - Literally, 'the next widest are called three fingers.' This phrase describes the second widest cutting rulers.
  • 又次等曰两指 - Literally, 'the next narrower are called two fingers.' This phrase describes the third widest cutting rulers.
  • 最狭者曰单指 - Literally, 'the narrowest are called single finger.' This phrase describes the narrowest cutting rulers.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 檇李 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in ancient China.
  • 顧淵白 - A person's name, likely a historical figure known for his talent and pride.
  • 元公復初 - A person's name, likely a historical figure known for his role in the court.
  • 燕都 - Refers to the capital city of the Yan state, which was an important city during the Warring States period.
  • 岳阳 - A place name, likely referring to a specific location in ancient China.
  • 高照庵先生 - A person's name, likely a historical figure known for his wisdom and teachings.
  • 裱背 - Refers to the art of mounting and backing paintings, a traditional Chinese craft.
  • 十三科 - Refers to the thirteen categories of work involved in the art of mounting and backing paintings.
  • 织造绫锦绢帛 - Refers to the process of weaving silk, brocade, and cloth, which were important materials in ancient China.
  • 染练上件 - Refers to the dyeing process for the fabrics mentioned.
  • 抄造纸劄 - Refers to the process of making paper, which was a significant technological advancement in ancient China.

  • 界尺裁版杆帖 - Refers to the tools used for cutting and pasting, which were necessary for the mounting process.
  • 轴头 - Refers to the axis heads used in mounting, which were often made of precious materials.
  • 糊刷 - Refers to the tool used for applying glue.

  • 防硬者谓之糊防 - Describes another type of glue brush.
  • 大小得中者谓之黏合 - Describes a third type of glue brush.
  • 狭小者谓之寸金 - Describes a fourth type of glue brush.

  • 次等曰三指 - Describes the second widest cutting rulers.
  • 又次等曰两指 - Describes the third widest cutting rulers.
  • 最狭者曰单指 - Describes the narrowest cutting rulers.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嘉定州大塲 - Refers to a specific location, likely a large market or trading area in Jia Ding Prefecture.
  • 下番买卖 - Literally means 'to go down to the sea for trade,' referring to maritime commerce.
  • 巨富 - Means 'extremely wealthy.'
  • 番船今到何处 - Refers to the arrival of a foreign ship and its current location.
  • 发金甲先囘 - '发' can mean 'to send' or 'to dispatch,' '金甲' refers to a person or object, and '先囘' means 'return first.' The phrase suggests that someone named '金甲' returned before others.
  • 碓坊甲头 - Likely refers to a person in charge of a rice mill or similar facility.
  • 首告 - Means 'to report first' or 'to inform first.'
  • 玉印未到 - Refers to a jade seal that had not yet arrived.
  • 沈厚赂官府 - Indicates that the Shen family bribed officials.
  • 蔺节妇 - Refers to a virtuous woman named Lin.
  • 许叔瑛【璞】云 - Indicates a statement or quote from Xu Shuying (also known as Xu Pu).
  • 陈友谅部属称邓平章者防江西某县 - Refers to a subordinate of Chen Youliang, a historical figure, who was in charge of a county in Jiangxi Province.
  • 防江西某县 - Means 'defended a certain county in Jiangxi Province.'
  • 因赂邓之帅某丐免剽戮 - Indicates that someone bribed a general to avoid being plundered and killed.
  • 帅贵人妾事之无恨 - Means 'the general is a noble person, and I serve him without resentment.'
  • 吾良人以礼币聘妾为妇者若干年 - Means 'my husband hired me as his wife with gifts for several years.'
  • 与生二子 - Means 'I gave birth to two sons.'
  • 妾不忍即背恩 - Means 'I cannot immediately betray my husband's kindness.'
  • 军中礼不备 - Means 'the military does not have the necessary rituals.'
  • 请持一月丧服乃为帅妇 - Means 'please allow me to wear mourning clothes for a month before becoming the general's wife.'
  • 帅许之 - Means 'the general agreed.'
  • 服未终移兵别县 - Means 'before the mourning period ended, the army moved to another county.'
  • 吾如汝约今夕谐吾婚乎 - Means 'I will follow your agreement tonight for our marriage.'
  • 诺 - Means 'yes' or 'agreed.'
  • 使二卒守蔺 - Means 'two soldiers were sent to guard Lin.'
  • 为取鸡酒具香火 - Means 'to prepare chicken, wine, incense, and candles.'
  • 今夕吾为帅妇敢告先良人灵 - Means 'tonight I will become the general's wife, and I dare to inform my late husband's spirit.'
  • 卒俱出乃先杀婴啮指血书壁 - Means 'the soldiers left, and she first killed her infant and bit her finger to write blood on the wall.'
  • 泾渭难分浊与清 - Means 'the clear and the turbid are hard to distinguish.'
  • 此身不幸厄红巾 - Means 'this body is unfortunate to be oppressed by the Red Turbans.'
  • 孤儿未忍更他姓 - Means 'I cannot bear to change the name of my orphaned child.'
  • 烈妇何曽嫁二人 - Means 'a brave woman has never married two men.'
  • 白刃自挥心似鐡 - Means 'she wielded the sword herself, her heart as hard as iron.'
  • 黄泉欲到骨如银 - Means 'she wanted to go to the netherworld, her bones as white as silver.'
  • 荒村日落猿啼处 - Means 'in a desolate village, the sun sets and apes cry.'
  • 过客闻之亦惨神 - Means 'passersby hear it and feel sorrowful.'
  • 书罢即自刎 - Means 'after writing, she immediately committed suicide.'
  • 帅返惊叹讯二卒欲罪之 - Means 'the general returned, amazed, and questioned the two soldiers, wanting to punish them.'
  • 卒指壁间题倩人读其诗驰白邓 - Means 'the soldiers pointed to the inscription on the wall, asking someone to read the poem and report it to Deng.'
  • 邓闻之陈陈为立庙旌表云 - Means 'Deng heard about it and established a temple to honor her.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉定州 - A prefecture in Zhejiang Province during the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 番船 - Refers to foreign ships, often associated with maritime trade.
  • 陈友谅 - A historical figure, a rebel leader during the late Yuan and early Ming Dynasties.
  • 忠孝里 - A place name, likely referring to a locality known for its loyalty and filial piety.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 番船今到何处发金甲先囘 - The phrase is somewhat ambiguous, but it suggests that a foreign ship arrived, and someone named '金甲' returned first.
  • 沈厚赂官府得理闻者亦可为戒 - The sentence structure is complex, but it indicates that the Shen family bribed officials to get their way, and others should take this as a warning.
  • 帅贵人妾事之无恨然吾良人以礼币聘妾为妇者若干年与生二子妾不忍即背恩军中礼不备请持一月丧服乃为帅妇未晚帅许之 - This long sentence describes Lin's decision to serve the general and her request to observe a mourning period before becoming his wife.
  • 卒俱出乃先杀婴啮指血书壁曰泾渭难分浊与清此身不幸厄红巾孤儿未忍更他姓烈妇何曽嫁二人白刃自挥心似鐡黄泉欲到骨如银荒村日落猿啼处过客闻之亦惨神书罢即自刎 - This complex sentence describes Lin's actions and the inscription she wrote before committing suicide.

Proper Nouns

  • 沈氏 - The Shen family.
  • 陈友谅 - A historical figure, a rebel leader during the late Yuan and early Ming Dynasties.
  • 邓平章 - A subordinate of Chen Youliang.
  • 蔺 - Lin, a virtuous woman.
  • 许叔瑛【璞】 - Xu Shuying (also known as Xu Pu), a historical figure.
  • 忠孝里 - A place name.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 至正壬辰秋七月 - Specific year and month according to the Chinese calendar (1352 AD, the seventh month of autumn).
  • 红巾防钱唐 - Refers to the Red Turban Rebellion, a significant uprising against the Yuan dynasty.
  • 乌合之众 - Literally 'a flock of crows,' used to describe a disorganized and ineffective group.
  • 坐累 - Means to suffer due to the actions of others, often translated as 'to be implicated.'
  • 潜聚无頼恶少 - Means to secretly gather worthless and wicked youths.
  • 资以饮食保防隣井 - Means to provide them with food and shelter to protect the neighborhood.
  • 悉殪而埋之 - Means to kill them all and bury them.
  • 素隐僻 - Means to be usually hidden and secluded.
  • 内应 - Means to act as an inside force aiding an attack or rebellion.
  • 赤心防国誓杀红巾八字作号 - Means to have the eight-character slogan 'Loyal Heart to Defend the Country, Vow to Kill the Red Turbans' inscribed on their backs.
  • 扶箕诗 - Refers to a type of divination poem written by a medium using a spirit board.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 至正 - The era name of Emperor Taiding of Yuan (1341-1368).
  • 壬辰 - The stem-branch notation for 1352 AD.
  • 红巾 - Refers to the Red Turban Rebellion, a major peasant uprising against the Yuan dynasty.
  • 钱唐 - The ancient name for Hangzhou, a city in Zhejiang province.
  • 吴兴延陵 - Refers to Wu County in Jiangsu province and Yanling, a town in Jiangsu.
  • 许晋字徳昭 - The name and courtesy name of Xu Jin, a local leader.
  • 如璋 - Xu Jin's son, also a brave warrior.
  • 浙东宣慰元帅观孙 - The name and title of Yuan army commander Guan Sun.
  • 扶箕诗 - A form of divination poetry, often associated with spiritual or supernatural practices.

Proper Nouns

  • 至正 - The era name of Emperor Taiding of Yuan.
  • 壬辰 - The stem-branch notation for 1352 AD.
  • 红巾 - The Red Turban Rebellion.
  • 钱唐 - Hangzhou.
  • 吴兴延陵 - Wu County and Yanling.
  • 许晋字徳昭 - Xu Jin, a local leader.
  • 如璋 - Xu Jin's son.
  • 浙东宣慰元帅观孙 - Guan Sun, a Yuan army commander.
  • 胡仲彬 - A storyteller in Hangzhou.
  • 扶箕诗 - A type of divination poem.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 書 - The Book of Documents.
  • 禮 - The Book of Rites.

Proper Nouns

  • 書 - The Book of Documents.
  • 禮 - The Book of Rites.

[607]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卷俱抄恐失手本之名字 - Copying the entire scroll to prevent the loss of the author's name.
  • 鼓勇於終塲之日 - Encouraging courage on the final day of the examination.
  • 局長之信已通 - The director's trust has been established.
  • 知名於未榜之前 - Became known before the announcement of the results.
  • 代筆之錢盡去 - All the money for proxy writing is gone.
  • 萬戶侯之關節 - The connections of a Marquis of Ten Thousand Households.
  • 可驗丈人峯之氣力 - Can verify the strength of Zhuren Peak.
  • 何勤 - Why be diligent?
  • 監門進樂平之八子 - The gatekeeper introduced eight sons from LePing.
  • 通括蒼之二林 - Connected with the two forests of Kuca.
  • 本生之地増輝 - The place of origin has been enhanced.
  • 同列之情不薄 - The feelings among colleagues are not shallow.
  • 稱干首二三月已買試官 - The head official was bought in February or March.
  • 在榜中十四名全頼妻父 - In the list, the fourteenth name was entirely dependent on his father-in-law.
  • 借開元真人之力 - Leveraged the power of the Immortal of the Kaiyuan Era.
  • 江郎兄弟 - The brothers of Jiang Lang.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 萬戶侯 - Refers to a high-ranking official, specifically a Marquis of Ten Thousand Households.
  • 丈人峯 - Likely a metaphor for someone's power or influence.
  • 樂平 - A place name, likely referring to a specific region or city.
  • 括蒼 - Another place name, possibly referring to a region or mountain range.
  • 開元真人 - Likely a reference to a Taoist figure or deity associated with the Kaiyuan Era.
  • 永嘉縣尹 - Refers to the magistrate of Yongjia County.
  • 江郎兄弟 - Likely a reference to the brothers of Jiang Lang.

  • 劉環 - Liu Huan, likely a person's name.
  • 環翁 - Huangong, likely a nickname or title.
  • 呂將 - Lü Jiang, likely a person's name.

  • 許道傅 - Xu Daofu, likely a person's name.
  • 戴用 - Dai Yong, likely a person's name.
  • 海魯丁 - Hai Luding, likely a person's name.
  • 吴县 - Wuxian, a county in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 括蒼 - Kuca, likely a place name.
  • 松慶彬祖 - Song Qingbinzu, likely a person's name.
  • 黄璋 - Huang Zhang, likely a person's name.
  • 松江 - Songjiang, a city in Shanghai, China.
  • 鲍恂 - Bao Xun, likely a person's name.
  • 嘉兴 - Jiaxing, a city in Zhejiang Province, China.
  • 俞鎮 - Yu Zhen, likely a person's name.

  • 叶瓉 - Ye Zhan, likely a person's name.
  • 信州 - Xinzhou, a city in Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 葉氏 - Ye family.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 頼为省郎之师 - Rely on as the teacher of the provincial official. This suggests a mentor-mentee relationship.
  • 运吏之壻 - Son-in-law of the official responsible for transportation. This indicates a familial and professional connection.
  • 托馆主有堂上之友 - Rely on the host of the academy to have friends among the elders. This suggests seeking support through connections within an educational institution.
  • 纷纷在眼歴歴难言 - Many things are in sight but hard to describe. This conveys a sense of complexity and difficulty in articulating the situation.
  • 作魁三百 - Made the top scorer out of three hundred. This indicates achieving the highest rank in an examination.
  • 定卖几千株之木 - Decided to sell several thousand trees. This suggests a business transaction involving a large number of trees.

[609]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 禮焉宜中 - Ritual propriety should be appropriate; emphasizes balance in ritual practices.
  • 錯春秋之年分 - Misalignment or confusion of the *Spring and Autumn Annals* with dates or events; may imply misinterpretation of historical records.
  • 亂周易之隂陽 - Disrupt the yin and yang of the *Zhou Yi*; suggests disturbance in cosmic balance or principles.
  • 心術之潛運者難知 - The subtle workings of the mind are hard to understand; refers to hidden or subconscious aspects of human thought.
  • 姑舍舉人更陳坐主 - Temporarily setting aside the candidates and focusing on the examiners or supervisors.
  • 夤縁考試 - To exploit the examination system; often has a negative connotation.
  • 豐卒嵗之赀 - To amass wealth for the end of the year; suggests financial gain or accumulation.
  • 買題登科 - To buy questions and pass the exam; implies corruption in the examination system.
  • 證舊時之本 - To prove or validate the original text; possibly refers to a canonical or authoritative version.
  • 易義驳雜 - The meaning of the *Yi* (Book of Changes) is mixed or confused; suggests lack of clarity or coherence in interpretation.
  • 名尋夏日之孜孜 - To seek the diligent efforts of summer; possibly refers to hard work or perseverance.
  • 分經考卷 - To divide the scriptures and exam papers; likely refers to organization or grading of exam materials.
  • 私情自開科 - To open the exam with personal bias or favoritism; suggests corruption in the examination process.
  • 出院改文 - To alter the text after leaving the exam; implies post-exam tampering or manipulation.
  • 欺公論 - To deceive public opinion; suggests a deliberate attempt to mislead or manipulate public perception.
  • 雖刋板乃是讹传 - Even though it was printed, it was still a false transmission; suggests printed materials can still contain errors or misinformation.
  • 歴观解据之非 - Throughout history, the flaws in the interpretations have been observed; indicates long-standing issues with the accuracy of interpretations.
  • 益见文衡之缪 - It becomes more evident that the standards of literary evaluation are flawed; critiques the examination system's criteria.
  • 指实告官者反罹其罪 - Those who report the truth to the authorities are instead punished; indicates a system where whistleblowers face consequences.
  • 仰天而叹息 - Looking to the sky and sighing; a common expression of frustration or despair.
  • 徒 - Only or merely; often used to express disappointment or futility.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 春秋 - Refers to the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, a historical text recording events of the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 周易 - Refers to the *Zhou Yi* (Book of Changes), one of the oldest Chinese classics used for divination and philosophical reflection.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 踏地以咨嗟 - Colloquial expression meaning 'to stamp one's feet and sigh,' indicating frustration or disappointment.
  • 聞夢吉陸居仁再来告免 - Direct translation of the names and action: 'Hearing the news that Meng Ji and Lu Juren came again to request exemption.'
  • 文运已矣 - Refers to a decline in literary prospects.
  • 吾道安之 - Expresses uncertainty about one's path or future.
  • 何等主司污滥壊今年之举 - Criticizes the corruption of the examination system.
